title: ERS claims file
description: Minimal claims-table format: atomic facts with provenance and conditional knowledge (P1, P2, P3 of ERS design v0.1). A claims file is a JSON object with a 'claims' array.

source_artifact.description: What is being checked (KB node id, document, file) — the text the claims were extracted from.

statement.description: One atomic proposition, independently true or false. Decompose compounds.

status.description: given = stated by the source artifact; derived = produced by a rule from other claims; assumed = temporary premise, withdrawable; hypothesis = one branch of an open alternative.

rule.description: Inference rule or procedure that produced this claim.

derived_from.description: REQUIRED when status=derived. Nothing appears without an explicit derivation.

hypothesis_deps.description: Ids of hypothesis/assumed claims this claim depends on. Derived claims inherit the union of their parents' deps; checker enforces closure.

contradicts.description: Ids of claims this claim is inconsistent with. Checker flags any pair where both are accepted (given/derived) on disjoint hypothesis branches... see checker for the exact rule.

evidence_class.description: How strongly the claim's verification stands. Upgrade only when the stronger form is in hand.

reproducer.description: Runnable artifact (file + command) that produces the verdict, or the literal string UNREPRODUCED. REQUIRED whenever evidence_class is present and not 'none'.

notes.description: Encoding choices, don't-cares, falsification framing ('what reading would make this row wrong').
